en: The issue is, if you're driving around and you're speeding, and you're doing it with a covered plate, you're never gonna get the ticket, and you're never even gonna realize how badly you're driving.
ko: 문제는 번호판 가리고 과속하면 딱지를 못 받고, 얼마나 위험하게 운전하는지도 모른다는 거야.

en: I wanna make sure the person gets all the tickets to which he's entitled.
ko: 그 사람이 받을 딱지를 다 받게 해주고 싶어.

en: What do you say to your critics who say, he's a snitch, he's a loser, he doesn't have a life, a hobby, a family, a job, he doesn't have friends or a social life?
ko: 비판하는 사람들이 밀고자, 루저, 취미도 가족도 친구도 없다고 하는데 뭐라고 하실 건가요?

en: What do you say to those people? I'm not trying to get people tickets. What I'm trying to do is show the people who are trying to get people tickets that their system is broken.
ko: 뭐라고 해요? 난 딱지를 끊으려는 게 아니야. 딱지 끊는 시스템이 고장났다는 걸 보여주는 거야.

en: We're in New York City, there's cops everywhere. Why aren't the police officers cracking down on these ghost plates?
ko: 뉴욕시에 경찰이 널렸는데 왜 유령 번호판을 단속 안 해요?

en: The vast majority of the people I've caught defacing or covering their plates are cops or firefighters or court officers.
ko: 내가 잡은 번호판 훼손자 대다수가 경찰, 소방관, 법원 경호원이었어.

en: I've done about 120 of these videos in the past three months, and I'd say about 70 of them were law enforcement officials.
The very people who shouldn't be breaking the law are finding a way to break the law by defacing their plate and then break the law by then speeding.
ko: 법을 지켜야 할 사람들이 번호판 훼손으로 법을 어기고, 과속으로 또 법을 어기는 거야.

en: You're getting more cops tickets for speeding. Give me some. That's fucking awesome.
ko: 경찰한테 과속 딱지를 더 끊게 하다니. 대박이네.

en: The goal is not to be an asshole. The goal is to be a little bit of an asshole. When does it end?
ko: 목표는 나쁜 놈 되는 게 아니야. 약간만 나쁜 놈이 되는 거지. 어디까지 갈 거야?

en: And does that mean you're in a ditch somewhere with a couple of NYPD bullets in your chest? Not that I want that.
ko: NYPD 총 맞고 도랑에 쓰러지는 거야? 바라는 건 아니지만.

en: I mean, it would make a great story. I actually see two heroes here. Yeah? I see you.
ko: 좋은 기사거리긴 하겠다. 여기 영웅이 두 명이야. 당신.

en: Thank you. Who is putting your life on the line. More or less. Enforcing law enforcement. And then I see another hero here, and that's me.
ko: 고마워요. 목숨 걸고 법 집행을 감시하는 사람. 그리고 또 한 명은 나야.

en: Because I found you, and I'm sharing your story with others. That's exactly right. So thank you for that.
ko: 당신을 찾아서 이야기를 공유하고 있으니까. 맞아요. 고마워요.

en: You can thank me later. I'll thank you right now. That's not what this segment's called.
ko: 나중에 감사하면 돼. 지금 할게요. 이 코너 이름이 그게 아닌데.
